findings.description: Header seal/logo graphics are missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF shows the North Carolina state seal centered at the top and the DEQ logo in the footer area; the HTML renders the text content but omits these graphics.

findings.description: Overall header layout is simplified and not fully preserved.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF has a three-part header with officials listed at upper left, seal centered, and date below; the HTML converts this into a single linear text flow, losing the original grouped header structure.

findings.description: Text styling and list indentation are weakened in the HTML.
findings.evidence: Across page 1, the PDF distinguishes underlined 'Certified Mail/Return Receipt Requested' and shows the numbered list with clearer indentation; the HTML keeps the text but with flatter styling and reduced visual distinction.

findings.description: Indented category labels and list structure are flattened somewhat in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF shows category labels like 'total and dissolved metals', 'microbiology', 'nutrients', 'wet chemistry', and 'bacteria' with strong indentation and spacing; the HTML presents them more like continuous paragraph text with less visual hierarchy.

findings.description: Basin itemization loses some visual separation in the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF presents each basin observation as a clearly separated line/block; the HTML preserves the wording but with reduced spacing and weaker distinction between items.

findings.description: Signature image is missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 4, the PDF includes a handwritten signature above 'Judith A. Wehner'; the HTML shows only typed text with no signature graphic.
